title: Follow-up: CORS-Test env-isolieren + `npm run lint` reparieren/entfernen (eslint fehlt komplett)
detail:
Zwei Tooling-/Test-Hygiene-Punkte, aufgetaucht waehrend iterate-2026-05-17-fix-resume-guard-survives-reload (PR #33, gemerged). Beide vorbestehend und unabhaengig vom resume-guard-Fix.

(a) CORS-Test env-sensitiv -- server/src/index.test.ts: der Test 'rejects Tailscale MagicDNS Origin in the default loopback-only policy' liest process.env.SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE ambient. Wenn die Dev-Shell SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=tailscale gesetzt hat, schlaegt der Test lokal fehl (1 von 1061); mit sauberer Env 6/6 gruen. Fix: SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE (+ verwandte Vars) im beforeEach stubben/clearen (vi.stubEnv), damit die Default-Policy-Assertions die Shell-Env nicht lesen. Klein (~10 LOC, eine Datei) -- eigenes trivial/small Iterate.

(b) 'npm run lint' ist tot -- client/package.json hat das Script 'eslint src/', aber WEDER eslint als devDependency NOCH irgendwo eine eslint-Config (kein eslint.config.* in client/server/root); server hat gar kein lint-Script. Das Script hat im Repo nie funktioniert (CLAUDE.md dokumentiert 'npm run lint # ESLint' als ob es ginge). Entscheidung noetig: (i) eslint flat-config + Plugins aufsetzen und den nie-gelinteten Findings-Backlog abarbeiten, (ii) auf oxlint umstellen (lief im Iterate sauber als Fallback), oder (iii) das tote lint-Script entfernen und CLAUDE.md korrigieren. Eigenes Tooling-Iterate.

title: Re-open a Done/Closed task — counterpart to Move-to-Backlog
detail: Counterpart direction to iterate-2026-05-17-move-to-backlog, which added In-Progress->Backlog only. Done is a terminal state with no path back. A follow-up iterate would let a user re-open a done task. Semantically heavier: in_progress is not a single settable state (it is derived: awaiting_external_start/active/idle/...). A re-open most likely targets draft (Backlog) so the user then relaunches, OR needs an explicit re-open endpoint. Pin the target state in that iterate interview. Builds on the /backlog endpoint pattern.

title: Serve the WebUI over HTTPS so terminal Ctrl+V paste works over Tailscale
detail: Follow-up to iterate-2026-05-18-terminal-copy-paste (PR #38), user-approved as a separate iterate during the copy/paste interview. That iterate added embedded-terminal keyboard copy/paste, but keyboard PASTE (Ctrl+V / Shift+Insert) cannot work when the WebUI is opened over the Tailscale IP: http on a non-loopback host is not a secure context, so the browser disables navigator.clipboard and document.execCommand(paste) is browser-blocked for web content. Copy works (the execCommand copy fallback works in a non-secure context); paste currently falls back to the browser native right-click -> Paste plus an inline hint. Fix: serve the WebUI over HTTPS so the origin becomes a secure context and Ctrl+V works natively. Likely tailscale serve or tailscale cert for the tailnet MagicDNS name; touches scripts/start-server-production.ps1 + the SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE / bind / TLS path. Deployment-layer change, deliberately not bundled into the copy/paste UI iterate. See conventions.md learning on navigator.clipboard secure-context gating.

title: ADR-103 retirement: split WS upgrade handler out of server/src/terminal/routes.ts
detail:
ADR-103 (2026-05-27) granted a bloat exception for server/src/terminal/routes.ts at 1013 LOC (state=exception, was=grandfathered). The Retirement Plan in ADR-103 names two candidate interventions to remove the exception:

1. **WS-upgrade-handler split (preferred candidate).** The onOpen(_evt, ws) callback is the largest single block in routes.ts (~400 LOC) and where the shared-locks argument is strongest (writer-slot + pause-refcount + attach-count + onData broadcast all interact). Extracting it into a dedicated module (e.g. server/src/terminal/ws-upgrade-handler.ts) would be a meaningful LOC reduction without forcing the per-handler-split anti-pattern ADR-103 explicitly rejects.
2. **Auth layer extraction.** If a future iterate adds task-attach auth (who is allowed to attach to which task), the writer-role decision could move out of the WS upgrade into a dedicated module.

**Re-Review-Date in ADR-103: 2026-08-27** (3 months). The reviewer at that date checks whether the seven-handler / shared-deps-bundle argument still holds. If neither retirement candidate has landed by then, the ADR should be re-evaluated or extended.

**Out of scope for retirement** (rejected in ADR-103): mechanical per-handler split (terminal-spawn-routes.ts / terminal-paste-routes.ts / ...) — that duplicates TerminalRoutesDeps plumbing OR extracts the bundle into a shared module, which is the failure mode ADR-101 calls out as 'moving complexity outward'.

**References:**
- .shipwright/planning/adr/103-bloat-exception-terminal-routes.md (full ADR)
- .shipwright/planning/adr/101-bloat-exception-pty-manager.md (sibling ADR — same template, also has a deferred retirement plan)
- shipwright_bloat_baseline.json: server/src/terminal/routes.ts entry (state=exception, adr=ADR-103, current=1013)

reason: Completed via PR #73 (C5 E2E backfill) + PR #75 (ptyReused prewarm-race fix) + PR #76 (followup: workaround removed). C5-embedded-terminal-split-smoke.spec.ts is on main, drives ADR-068-A1 auto-execute end-to-end (Launch → ready{role:writer,ptyReused:false} → 250ms quiesced WS data-frame; reload → ptyReused:true). Resume-click→no-duplicate-launch deferred per spec — needs Claude JSONL bootstrap which isolated USERPROFILE prevents; covered by 204 EmbeddedTerminal.test.tsx unit cases.
Implemented in PR #77 (commit a662027) — server/src/terminal/ws-upgrade-handler.ts extracted; routes.ts 1013 -> 620 LOC. ADR-103 Retirement Status updated.

title: ADR-103 retirement candidate #2 + Re-Review-Date 2026-08-27 (terminal/routes.ts still 620 LOC, exception still active)
detail:
Follow-up to PR #77 (commit a662027, merged 2026-05-27 as 560b971). ADR-103 retirement candidate #1 (WS-upgrade-handler split) PARTIALLY landed: server/src/terminal/routes.ts dropped 1013 → 620 LOC, but the exception is NOT fully retired because the file still exceeds the 300-LOC default (HTTP route handlers + spawn-env factory remain). Two open items remain on ADR-103:

1. **Retirement candidate #2 — Auth layer extraction.** If/when task-attach auth (who is allowed to attach to which task) lands, the writer-role decision could move out of the WS upgrade into a dedicated module — that would be a meaningful further reduction. Currently speculative; do NOT mechanically extract without a real auth requirement (would be 'moving complexity outward', the failure mode ADR-101/103 explicitly reject).

2. **Re-Review-Date 2026-08-27** (3 months out from ADR creation). On that date, the reviewer checks whether the seven-handler / shared-deps-bundle argument still holds for routes.ts AND ws-upgrade-handler.ts. If neither retirement candidate has landed by then, ADR-103 should be re-evaluated or extended.

**References:**
- .shipwright/planning/adr/103-bloat-exception-terminal-routes.md (§Retirement Status updated 2026-05-27)
- .shipwright/planning/iterate/2026-05-27-ws-upgrade-handler-split.md (PR #77 iterate spec)

title: SmartViewer pop-out: replace new-tab with a centered modal
detail: SmartViewer Pop-out button currently opens /preview in a NEW BROWSER TAB (window.open). User wants a centered in-app MODAL instead. Design: reuse the Radix Dialog pattern (NewIssueModal/ModalShell) - viewport-centered overlay (portal to body, dimmed full-app backdrop, NOT anchored to the pane), size min(1200px,92vw) x 90vh, header with file path + close X, ESC/backdrop close; body renders SmartViewer with its own scroll. Suppress the nested pop-out button inside the modal (thread popOut=false through SmartViewer -> MarkdownRenderer). CLIENT-ONLY -> deploys via client rebuild, no server restart. Do via /shipwright-iterate (small UI -> F0.5 E2E: modal opens centered, ESC closes, SmartViewer renders inside). Keep the /preview route as-is.

title: Deploy: restart prod server to activate PR open/merged badge route
detail: iterate-2026-05-30-pr-card-status merged a new server route GET /api/external/pr-status (gh-backed PR open/merged status), but the running production server (port 3847, node dist/index.js) was NOT restarted (deferred to protect running embedded sessions). Until a server rebuild + process restart, transcript PR cards show the new bubble width but NO open/merged badge (client gets 404 -> unknown -> no badge, graceful). To deploy when embedded sessions are idle: run scripts/start-server-production.ps1 (builds server+client + stop-old/start-fresh) OR npm --prefix server run build then restart the node process. Restart kills embedded-terminal ptys (resumable via Resume button); external terminal sessions unaffected. All other client UI changes are already deployed live.

detail: The campaign lane (proposed-campaigns-board-lane.md) currently has only the per-step Launch-next action. Add a SECOND lane action that fires one command into the embedded terminal: /shipwright-iterate --campaign <slug> --autonomous. Resume-awareness is ALREADY solved in shared/scripts/lib/autonomous_loop.py (_load_units_from drops complete/done units; cmd_init resumes pending; reconciliation from result.json), so the button needs NO progress logic — it just runs the remaining pending sub-iterates in order from status.json. Guardrails to include: (1) a confirm dialog (autonomous = no gates, unlike the harmless per-step launch); (2) warn-or-disable when a pending sub-iterate is flagged plan-first/risky (e.g. campaign 2026-06-02-hook-consolidation B1 must never run unattended). Reuses the same terminal-injection plumbing as the per-step Launch. Phase-2 follow-up to the lane spec.

title: Campaigns lane: a COMPLETED campaign should disappear on its own, not linger until EVERY campaign is complete
detail: The Campaigns lane (FR-01.33) currently hides only when EVERY campaign is complete (or the dir is empty). A campaign that is itself 100%% done still renders as a card as long as ANY other campaign is incomplete -- e.g. 2026-06-02-compliance-detective-realign (4/4, lifecycle=complete) stays visible only because 2026-06-02-hook-consolidation is 0/7 draft. From the user's POV this reads as a bug: a closed campaign should go away. Expected: filter campaigns where done==total (or lifecycle=='complete') out of the default lane view -- optionally behind a collapsed 'Completed (N)' toggle -- so the lane surfaces only active work. Affects server/src/core/campaign-store.ts (or the client lane filter) + client/src/components/external/CampaignLaneCard.tsx / useCampaigns. Reported 2026-06-05 while closing the compliance-detective-realign campaign.

title: [Migration] Track .shipwright/triage.jsonl SSoT - gitignore + GATED GC of ~101-item pile (full runbook + autonomy in detail; mirror monorepo E)
detail:
RUNBOOK for migrating webui's triage backlog to a git-tracked SSoT. Plugin cache is on v0.24.0, all tools ready.

LAUNCH (paste into a FRESH Claude session opened in THIS shipwright-webui repo): /shipwright-iterate change "Migrate .shipwright/triage.jsonl to the git-tracked SSoT, mirroring monorepo sub-iterate E. Follow the runbook in this triage card (gitignore migration + GATED destructive GC)."

WHY: webui's .shipwright/triage.jsonl (~101 lines, REAL curated backlog) is gitignored via the /.shipwright/* wildcard with NO negation, so the committed snapshot diverges from webui's own Command Center -- exactly the divergence the monorepo campaign 2026-06-05-track-triage-jsonl closed.

PROCEDURE (4 steps):
1) gitignore (non-destructive): run gitignore_canon.py --project-root . (adds the canonical block incl. the !/.shipwright/triage.jsonl negation), then scaffold_triage_inbox.py --project-root . (self-heal: strip any stale bare ignore line, keep .lock, add .bak). Verify: `git check-ignore .shipwright/triage.jsonl` returns empty (now trackable).
2) GATED DESTRUCTIVE GC -- DO NOT SKIP THE GATE:
   a. triage_gc.py --project-root . (dry-run is the default) -> SHOW total / droppable (machine-churn) / kept, broken down by reason.
   b. >>> OPERATOR GATE: confirm the dry-run before --apply. <<<
   c. triage_gc.py --project-root . --apply (writes .bak, atomic rewrite, re-validates).
   d. VERIFY the diff vs .bak BEFORE committing -- HARD invariants, ABORT if ANY fails: (i) removed-set is a subset of dismissed; (ii) 0 open/promoted lost; (iii) 0 status-change on any kept item; (iv) churn_merge.validate_triage_text returns 0 errors. (These are exactly what made monorepo E provably safe.)
3) regenerate triage_inbox.md from the canonical jsonl (aggregate_triage.py --project-root .) -> open-count must equal webui's Command Center.
4) commit (.gitignore + .shipwright/triage.jsonl + triage_inbox.md) + open a PR. Do NOT auto-merge -- review the diff first; that PR review is the final human gate.

AUTONOMY (the operator's question -- "can I run this unattended?"): steps 1/3/4 are autonomous-safe. Step 2 is DESTRUCTIVE.
- SEMI-AUTO (RECOMMENDED): keep the operator gate at 2b. You are in the loop ~10s. Constitution-compliant (destructive ops are ASK-FIRST even in autonomous mode).
- UNATTENDED: the 2b human gate MAY be replaced by the 2d hard-invariant asserts (abort on any violation) -- but NEVER skip both. The .bak (rollback) plus the un-merged PR are the safety net. webui's dismissals are real human curation (via the Command Center), so the dry-run MUST be inspected -- by the operator (2b) or by the 2d asserts -- before --apply lands.
- DO NOT just commit the pile un-GC'd: that bakes ~53 machine-churn items into webui history forever (defeats the A+B purpose of the campaign).

REFERENCE: monorepo E spec at .shipwright/planning/iterate/campaigns/2026-06-05-track-triage-jsonl/sub-iterates/E-triage-docs-monorepo-migration.md (in the shipwright repo); reference implementation = monorepo commit e52985bc (PR #160).

title: [monorepo] A5.6 dormant-trigger: add a5_phase_b_activated opt-in (deliberate Phase B is a false-positive)
detail:
PRODUCER-SIDE FIX (shipwright monorepo, NOT webui).

WHERE: plugins/shipwright-compliance/.../scripts/audit/group_a5.py (_check_a5_6_triggers, _Convention, _apply_overrides) + shared/scripts/lib/security_workflow.py.

WHAT: add an opt-in config key 'a5_phase_b_activated' (bool). Read it in _apply_overrides from audit_config.json and carry it on _Convention. When true, _check_a5_6_triggers treats active pull_request/schedule as PASS, while STILL enforcing (a) workflow_dispatch presence and (b) the bare-'on:'/no-triggers failure. Only the 'non-dormant trigger active' sub-case is waived, never the structural guards.

THEN (webui): set a5_phase_b_activated:true in audit_config.json (next to the existing g2_stoplist) so A5.6 goes green.

WHY: webui's security.yml + codeql.yml are deliberately Phase B (commit 7196205 'activate Security Scan on PRs + weekly schedule'; codeql d66ab55). The check has no way to record deliberate activation -> permanent LOW false-positive. audit_config.disabled_checks:[A5.6] is too blunt (also drops the workflow_dispatch-presence guard).

ACCEPTED meanwhile in webui via decision drop run_id iterate-2026-06-07-a5-6-dormant-accepted (-> ADR at /shipwright-changelog). Do NOT change any webui workflow triggers.

title: Campaign autonomous loop never marks the running sub-iterate in_progress (status.json)
detail: Producer follow-up to webui iterate-2026-06-08-campaign-attached-run-guard (side b shipped). campaign-mode.md step 3g calls campaign_progress update-status only with --status complete; it never marks the CURRENT sub-iterate in_progress before spawning the runner, so the WebUI board reads done/total=0/N for the whole build (looks 'not started') then jumps to 1/N. FIX (a): in the autonomous loop, call campaign_progress update-status --status in_progress for the unit BEFORE the sub-iterate-runner spawns (status already in the choices). Lives in the shipwright MONOREPO (plugins/shipwright-iterate skills .../references/campaign-mode.md + scripts/tools/campaign_progress.py) — NOT this webui repo. The webui consumer already renders in_progress steps and unions a status.json in_progress signal into attachedRun, so this producer write makes the per-step feedback + the secondary attached-run signal light up.

title: WebUI live triage view must union triage.jsonl ∪ triage.outbox.jsonl
detail: Follow-up to MONOREPO campaign 2026-06-08-triage-outbox-delivery (anchor trg-94f70926). Once D1 moves background producers to the gitignored per-tree outbox, the WebUI live Inbox (reads the tracked triage.jsonl) will NOT show background findings until the next sweep+merge -> regression. WebUI server-side triage reader must union tracked ∪ outbox (TS). Also verify the propagated monorepo fix actually reaches this repo (Codex Q6: 'shared/ change != deployed'). Separate iterate; run AFTER D1-D3 land + propagate. Was campaign sub-iterate D4 but the autonomous campaign loop is per-repo, so it cannot run in the monorepo campaign.

title: Campaign/Triage launch CTAs have no running-guard + loop never writes in_progress
detail: Two related gaps in the campaign launch surface. (1) DOUBLE-LAUNCH FOOTGUN: CampaignLaneCard.tsx renders 'Launch (Cx)' + 'Launch autonomous' (and the triage-card 'Start Campaign' CTA) with NO 'a run is already attached' guard — only disabled when there is no launchable step/project. A second click while an autonomous orchestrator is already attached spawns a SECOND orchestrator on the same campaign -> racing worktrees/commits + corrupted status.json. (2) NO IN-PROGRESS FEEDBACK: the autonomous loop (campaign-mode.md step 3g) calls campaign_progress update-status only with --status complete; it never marks the running sub-iterate in_progress. The card CAN render in_progress (CampaignLaneCard.tsx ~L178) but nothing writes it, so the board sits at done/total=0/N with the next-marker on the running step — visually identical to 'not started' for the entire build, then jumps to 1/N. Observed 2026-06-08: campaign 2026-06-08-triage-outbox-delivery launched (orchestrator session 079ad1b5 prepping D1), board stuck at 0/4 with both Launch buttons live. FIX (small, two-sided): (a) loop marks the current sub in_progress before spawning the runner (update-status --status in_progress; status already in choices); (b) card disables/relabels the launch CTAs (and the triage Start-Campaign CTA) when any sub is in_progress / a run is attached. Also consider surfacing loop_state.json / loop.lock as the attached-run signal.

title: WebUI-originated triage appends bypass the outbox: manual cards write straight to tracked triage.jsonl on idle main
detail: Residual of campaign 2026-06-08-triage-outbox-delivery (D4 closed by webui PR #120 + monorepo PR #177/#182): D1 rerouted triage_add + idle-main background producers to the gitignored per-tree triage.outbox.jsonl, but APPENDS originated by the WebUI itself (manual issue/launch cards via the server write path) still land directly in the tracked .shipwright/triage.jsonl on idle main. Every such append (and the residence-derived status flips that follow tracked appends, e.g. the 2 webui dismissals folded as chore(triage) 4a2bf49 on 2026-06-10) re-creates uncommitted main-tree drift -> fold commits orphan on local main (the J2 ahead-count pattern PR #172 solved for background producers). FIX SKETCH: route webui-originated NEW appends through the outbox too (core/triage-write.ts append path, residence-derivation already exists for status events); they become visible immediately via the union reader and now carry the pending-delivery badge (PR #120), and the next iterate's setup sweep delivers them into that PR. Do NOT change residence-derived status flips for tracked appends (by design - a status must never orphan from its append, D1 doubt-review HIGH). DECISION 2026-06-10: file only, do NOT start the iterate yet (user will schedule).

reason: False-positive (code-verified 2026-06-10): the webui server has NO triage-append path — triage-write.ts writes only event:status, never a new item. The cited drift (webui dismissals landing in tracked) is the by-design TRACKED-PREFERRED residence rule, handled by reconcile_main_triage. CLI triage_add is already outbox-routed (this item itself landed in the outbox = D1 working). Nothing to build; diagnosis rested on an imprecise legacy memory note, not the code.

title: Campaign board: migrate consumer from status.json to event-log projection
detail: The WebUI server reads campaign status from .shipwright/planning/iterate/campaigns/<slug>/status.json (server/src/core/campaign-status-json.ts + campaign-store.ts). Campaign planning dirs are now gitignored/local-only (monorepo PR #189 + webui PR #121), so a clone/redeploy has no campaign dirs -> the Campaigns board shows nothing on a redeployed instance (a local working-tree instance still works). Migrate the consumer to project campaign status from the tracked events.jsonl via the campaign+sub_iterate_id event extras (monorepo shared/scripts/lib/campaign_status.py::project_campaign_status, ADR-121). Then gitignored campaigns still surface progress on a deployed board.
